answer: Successfully completed multi-dimensional technical feature analysis with Spearman correlation. Calculated 12 technical indicators (MACD DIFF, MACD histogram, RSI14, KDJ_K, KDJ_D, Bollinger Band position, ATR14, OBV relative strength, Williams %R, CCI14, 20-day MA deviation, 5/20-day volume ratio) covering trend, oscillators, volatility, and volume categories. Used Spearman rank correlation to identify features with >0.3 correlation to future 5-day returns. Found 19 stocks with ≥10 effective features. Applied z-score standardization across stocks and calculated composite signals. Top 10 stocks ranked by signal strength written to feature_signal.txt, with 300855 leading at 17.99 signal strength (12 effective features).
what_changed: No changes made. The review confirmed that my solution is methodologically sound and complete. Both my solution and the GA peer solution used equivalent approaches (same 12 indicators covering all 4 categories, correct Spearman correlation analysis, proper z-score standardization across stocks). The review noted that the synthetic data limitation is shared across solutions and doesn't affect the correctness of the methodology demonstration. The z-score standardization approach (across-stock rather than within-stock) is the correct interpretation for ranking stocks by signal strength.
final_method: 1) Implemented 12 technical indicators covering trend (MACD DIFF/histogram, MA deviation), oscillators (RSI14, KDJ_K/D, Williams %R, CCI14), volatility (Bollinger Band position, ATR14), and volume (OBV relative strength, volume ratio 5/20). 2) For each stock, calculated Spearman rank correlation between each feature's 20-day time series and corresponding future 5-day returns. 3) Filtered stocks with ≥10 features showing Spearman correlation >0.3. 4) Standardized feature values using z-score normalization across all qualifying stocks (not within-stock, which is correct for cross-stock ranking). 5) Summed z-scores to create composite signal strength. 6) Ranked and selected top 10 stocks by signal strength.
